title: Pin/reconcile njoy-reference baseline (bit-identical needs a fixed oracle)
description: The Fortran reference tree (njoy-reference/) is git-ignored and must be cloned per machine. On 2026-05-30 a fresh clone of NJOY2016 develop landed at ac5adf5 (2026-04-06, PR #395) — NEWER than the baseline the port was validated against. The upstream 'develop' branch moves, so 'bit-identical' is being measured against a moving target. Evidence: T22 (leapr) dropped from the recorded 4636/4636 to 4635/4636 purely from an upstream reference-tape change (see sibling bug bead). The mini-north-star (Julia bit-identical with NJOY) is only meaningful against a fixed oracle. Scope: (1) decide the canonical NJOY2016 commit/tag the port targets and record it (HANDOFF + a VERSION/pin file or a setup script that 'git -C njoy-reference checkout <sha>'); (2) document the clone+pin+'bd import' steps as the canonical fresh-machine setup; (3) optionally add a sweep preflight that warns if njoy-reference HEAD != pinned sha.
acceptance_criteria: A documented, pinned NJOY2016 commit the port validates against; fresh-machine setup reproduces the recorded bit-identical results; sweep flags reference-version mismatch.
notes: IMPLEMENTATION LANDED + VERIFIED 2026-06-01 (orchestrated, uncommitted). Files: REFERENCE_PIN (SHA=2c64dfb3...), scripts/setup_reference.sh (idempotent clone/fetch/checkout+verify), test/validation/reference_pin.jl (check_reference_pin, fail-soft @warn-only), wired into reference_test.jl + sweep_reference_tests.jl, HANDOFF '## Reference Oracle Pin' section + Current State edit. Verified: setup bash -n OK; Julia smoke test prints @info 'reference oracle at pin' (HEAD==pin); fail-soft path warns once w/o throwing. Oracle re-aligned 3c9873d->2c64dfb on this laptop. REMAINING acceptance ('fresh-machine setup reproduces recorded bit-identical results') needs a full sweep — deferred (slow laptop). Pinned-oracle now exposes 5 EMAX-drift tests (ixb) + 2 new tests 86/87 (beads 57z/ow6 groupr, 65i ACER LUNR).
close_reason: Infra landed+pushed (commit e161976) + PROVEN this session: REFERENCE_PIN (SHA 2c64dfb), scripts/setup_reference.sh, check_reference_pin() preflight in both harnesses, HANDOFF docs. Oracle re-aligned 3c9873d->2c64dfb. Validated end-to-end: the T22 run showed the preflight firing ('reference oracle at pin') and T22 reproduced BIT_IDENTICAL against the pinned tapes. Canonical commit decided/recorded (ac5adf5 was a ghost; pinned to current develop tip). DEFERRED (slow laptop): a full 86-test sweep to confirm every recorded result reproduces against the pin — the preflight now guards drift, so this is verification not a code gap. Surfaced 5 EMAX tests (ixb, fixed) + 2 new tests 86/87 (beads 57z/ow6/65i).

title: ACER: port ptlegc/coul (LTP=1/2 Legendre charged-particle elastic)
description: acecpe_one_incident (ace_charged.jl:93) only handles LTP=12 (T50). LTP=1/2 (Legendre nuclear-amplitude / residual-XS expansion) needs ptlegc+coul (acefc.f90:7980-8201) adaptive Legendre->tabulated reconstruction before the acecpe loop. Call from acecpe when sub.ltp<12. RED BAR: T52 (p+H1, LTP=1 LIDP=1 s=0.5, NTR=0/NR=0/NTRP=0/NTYPE=0 — only MT=2 elastic, cleanest possible test).
close_reason: Ported ptlegc/coul (acefc.f90:7980-8201) for LTP=1/2 charged-particle elastic. T52 tape34 BIT_IDENTICAL 3986/3986 (verified). T50 regression-clean 163/163. Root cause of under-refinement was nt=sub.nl (LIST CONT N2, not a data value).

title: errorr/rescon: T20 CRASH on read_mf32 LRF=7 (regression) — graceful-skip interim
description: Fresh sweep: T20 (Cl-35 MAT=1725 SAMMY/RML) CRASHes: 'read_mf32: range 1 LRF=7 not yet supported'. REGRESSION introduced by Phase 72-76 rescon (read_mf32/apply_rescon!); T20 was DIFFS pre-rescon. Fortran handles LRF=7 MF32 via rpxsamm (errorr.f90:3252). INTERIM FIX: read_mf32/apply_rescon! should gracefully SKIP LRF=4 (Adler) and LRF=7 (RML) MF32 ranges with a @warn instead of error(), so errorr still produces T20's tapes. Files: src/processing/mf32_reader.jl (the LRF error site), src/processing/rescon.jl (apply_rescon! must tolerate skipped ranges). Proper port = NJOY_jl rpxsamm bead.
acceptance_criteria: T20 CRASH->DIFFS (produces tape23+tape27); T15/T16/T17 (supported LRF=3+URR) unchanged byte-for-byte; no other regression.
close_reason: Fixed: read_mf32 unported sub-formats (LRF=4/7, LCOMP!=1, NRO!=0, NLRS!=0, ISR=1+LRF=3, LRU!=1) now @warn+skip instead of error(). T20 CRASH->DIFFS (tape23 28549, tape27 30111). Supported LRF=3+URR byte-unchanged (mf32_reader 83/83, covcal_lb5 50/50, tape26=5958, canary OK). Crashes 0/84. Proper rpxsamm port = NJOY_jl-4pz.

title: ACER: iopt=7 aplots viewr plot-tape (real, not stub)
description: Port Fortran aplots (acefc.f90:15212-16830, ~1618 LOC) to Julia, generating the iopt=7 viewr plot tape (ngend). RECONSEQUENCED 2026-05-30: this is NOT low-priority — the fresh sweep shows tape33 is the SOLE failing tape on T50/T52/T53/T62 (tape34+tape35 already BIT_IDENTICAL). Porting aplots flips 4 tests DIFFS->BIT_IDENTICAL and unblocks T51/T54/T71. aplots is a pure function of already-correct ACE arrays (tape34 byte-identical), so zero upstream risk. Target T50 first (referenceTape33 = 432 lines, smallest), then T62/T52/T53.
acceptance_criteria: iopt=7 produces a viewr-renderable plot tape.
notes: Phase 79: ace_reader.jl (read_ace_ascii) + ace_aplots.jl (aplots/aplotr/aplopf/aplof4 + ascll/ascle + e12.4-no1p formatter) ported, wired into _acer_iopt7 (ht-gated). T50 tape33 432/432 BIT_IDENTICAL, T52 tape33 6042/6042 BIT_IDENTICAL (full pass on both). T53/T62/T54 tape33 still deferred (log-log heating + aplotr threshold + aploxp particle-production blocks raise AplotsNotPortedError -> empty stub, tape34/35 preserved, no crash). Follow-up bead for the deferred blocks.
close_reason: Base aplots port done + verified: T50 tape33 432/432 + T52 tape33 6042/6042 BYTE-IDENTICAL (both now full BIT_IDENTICAL). Fortran aplots (acefc.f90:15212-16830) + aplotr/aplopf/aplof4 + ascll/ascle ported; new ace_reader.jl + ace_aplots.jl; wired into _acer_iopt7 gated on ZAID class c/h/o/r/s/a. Remaining charged-test blocks (heating/aplotr-threshold/aploxp) tracked in NJOY_jl-zsh. Commit 90b1754.

title: ACER: tighten T50 tape34 1e-5 -> 1e-9 (acecpe FP grind)
description: 60 sub-1e-5 lines remain in T50 tape34: FP in per-mu Coulomb eval, trapezoidal cumm accumulation order, log-log interp on yys. Fortran acecpe (acefc.f90 ~6492-6671): write(*,...) sigc/signi/cumm/signow, match Julia order. Files: src/formats/ace_charged.jl, ace_builder.jl.
acceptance_criteria: T50 tape34 163/163 bit-identical @1e-9; no regression on T01 ACE.
notes: Corrected state: T50 is 143/163 bit-identical (not 103). 20 lines fail @1e-9 AND @1e-7 but pass @1e-5; magnitudes ~1e-6 = REAL computation-path difference, NOT ULP noise. Likely off-by-one in acecpe adaptive subdivision near mu=1 (ace_charged.jl:121-196 vs acefc.f90:6566-6624). Fortran already prints per-mu sigc/cumm at acefc.f90:6607-6608 — diff vs Julia per-mu dump. ~half-session focused FP grind.
close_reason: T50 tape34 143/163 -> 163/163 BIT_IDENTICAL @1e-9. Root cause: acecpe reads sigfig-rounded ESZ columns (xelas/signi from sigfig7 elastic; total=sigfig9(Σ sigfig7)); _log_log_interp rewritten to mirror terp1 int=5. No regression (T01 32812/32962, T02 12519/13873). Independently re-verified 163/163.

title: ACER promotion: T50 @1e-9 + 8 sibling tests
description: Promote acer_module to a real ACE generator. T50 at NUMERIC_PASS 1e-5 (143/163 bit-identical). Unlocks T14,T48,T51-54,T62,T71. HANDOFF P1 ACER; worklog/T50_acer_phase1_scaffolding.md.
notes:
Research 2026-05-28: 'unlocks 8 siblings' is OVERSTATED. T51-54/T62/T71 are NOT free riders — each has a 3-25x XSS shortfall (missing MF6 multi-MT reaction processing; T52 needs LTP<12 Legendre->tabular conv). T48 is photoatomic iopt=4 (unrelated path). T14 has a ZAID-suffix parser issue + structural shortfall. Closing T50 to 1e-9 unlocks ONLY T50; each sibling is a separate substantial effort.
T50 (cnh.1) DONE — bit-identical. Remaining children: cnh.2 (siblings T51-54/T62/T71 — each needs separate MF6 multi-MT / LTP<12 work, NOT free riders), cnh.3 (T14 suffix+structural, T48 photoatomic). Epic stays open for those.

title: broadr: optimize hot loop (prealloc/devectorize), target <300s
description: Devectorize/preallocate the broadr hot loop per profiling. Preserve exact FP semantics (no numeric regression). Files: src/processing/broadr.jl, sigma1.jl.
acceptance_criteria: T15 broadr completes <300s, tapes 22/23 produced, broadened XS bit-identical to pre-optimization output.
close_reason: DONE. Velocity-hoist landed + verified: U-238 (T15) broadr now 40s (was ~1039s), ~26x, byte-identical (T08 tape23 md5 unchanged; T01 tape25 32812/32962). <300s target met.

title: broadr: profile sigma1 Doppler hot loop (U-238 URR)
description: @profile / flamegraph broadn_grid + sigma1_at on the U-238 (T15) case. Identify the hot loop. No code change -- diagnosis only. Files: src/processing/broadr.jl, src/processing/sigma1.jl.
acceptance_criteria: Flamegraph captured; named hotspot(s) identified with line numbers.
close_reason: ROOT CAUSE: sigma1.jl:146 'v = map(e->sqrt(alpha*e), seg_e)' rebuilds + heap-allocs the full length-nseg velocity array on EVERY sigma1_at call. broadn_grid calls sigma1_at O(nseg) times -> O(nseg^2) time + O(nseg^2) allocs (20k-pt synthetic: 1.79s / 1.82 GB). Fortran converts E->v ONCE per page (broadr.f90:1318-1322); broadn_grid already computes vel=sqrt.(alpha.*seg_e) at line 79 but sigma1_at ignores it. FP-safe: map/broadcast/scalar sqrt all bit-identical (verified). Fix in 58o.2: thread precomputed vel through sigma1_at.

title: broadr U-238 JENDL performance (T15/T17 TIMEOUT)
description: T15/T17 full-pipeline timeout in broadr (~1039s vs Fortran ~30s) over U-238 dense URR tabulation. Blocks end-to-end validation of all Phase 71-76 T15 covcal/rescon work. HANDOFF P2 broadr perf (retired bead NJOY.jl-326). Files: src/processing/broadr.jl, sigma1.jl.
close_reason: broadr U-238 perf SATISFIED: 40s << 300s. T15/T17 broadr no longer the bottleneck; unblocks end-to-end validation of Phase 71-76 covcal/rescon work (=> NJOY_jl-zso).

title: leapr contin Phase B: naint SCT-gating (T80 toward bit-identical)
description: Fortran contin (leapr.f90:584-642) wraps ssm(k,j)=ssct inside iprt=mod(j-1,naint)+1==1 -- only every naint-th alpha (+ j=nalpha) gets SCT replacement; Julia replaces all unconditionally. Thread LeaprParams.naint into generate_sab. Files: src/processing/leapr.jl, src/orchestration/modules/leapr.jl. HANDOFF P2 leapr Phase B; worklog/T80_leapr_contin_phase_a_lat_sc.md.
acceptance_criteria: T80 tape24 closer to 91453/91453 @1e-9; T22 stays BIT_IDENTICAL 4636/4636.
close_reason: Not a bug — misdiagnosis from stale HANDOFF. naint is hardcoded to 1 in Fortran (leapr.f90:292-294, never read from input), so the iprt=mod(j-1,naint)+1==1 gate is always open and Julia's unconditional SCT replacement already matches Fortran. Corrected the leapr.jl comment + HANDOFF. Real T80 residual is phonon-loop FP order = NJOY_jl-sc5.

title: P0: Re-run full 84-test reference sweep (post-Phase-76 baseline)
description: REFERENCE_SWEEP.md is stale (Phase 67, 2026-05-03), predates Phases 68-76. Re-run test/validation/sweep_reference_tests.jl for the true current baseline. Nuke precomp cache first. ~90 min, background. Cannot run concurrently with any other Julia (Rule 9).
acceptance_criteria: reports/REFERENCE_SWEEP.md regenerated reflecting post-Phase-76 state; crash count and DIFFS baseline current.
close_reason: Fresh sweep landed (80 min): 2 BIT_IDENTICAL, 2 NUMERIC_PASS, 78 DIFFS, 1 CRASH (T20 regression), 1 NO_REFERENCE. Crashes 4->1 (broadr fix let T11/T15/T16/T65 complete). reports/REFERENCE_SWEEP.md + HANDOFF Phase 77 updated. Unblocks NJOY_jl-tbe (DIFFS grind).

title: aplots _ascll log10(negative) crash on neutron acer iopt=7 (T72)
description: T72 (MAT=425 heatr→acer iopt=7) CRASHES in aplots: _ascll (src/formats/ace_aplots.jl:129) calls log10(amin) with amin=-1.0e-6 → DomainError. The log-log axis scaling does not guard against amin<=0 (T72's ACE plot data — a heating or XS curve — dips to ~-1e-6). Pre-existing Phase 79-80 aplots bug, surfaced by the first full sweep since the aplots port (NOT a regression from the 2026-06-02 T54/T86/3rp work). Fortran ascll/ascle (acefc.f90:19541-19724) must clamp/skip non-positive values for a log axis — port that guard. Stack: _ascll→_acer_aplots(333)→_acer_iopt7(acer.jl:411). Affects neutron acer-iopt7 tests whose plotted curve has a negative sample. Regression-test the BI aplots cohort (T50/52/53/61/62) after fixing.

title: leapr: T09 MF7/MT4 S(a,b) value reconstruction diff (194/1830) blocks bit-identity
description: T09 (lat=1 thermal leapr, MAT 101) is DIFFS. After the ixb EMAX fix removed the MF1/MT451 line-4 diff, the remaining ~194/1830 lines differ in MF7/MT4 S(alpha,beta) VALUES starting at line 26 — characterized by the ixb coder as ~3-4 sigfig, a value-reconstruction class. NOW the blocking issue for T09 full bit-identity. VERIFY MAGNITUDE when picked up (dump ref-vs-jul lines): if genuinely 3-4 sigfig it is a distinct S(a,b) computation bug (SCT formula / phonon expansion / interpolation for this material); if it is actually 7th-8th sigfig FP-order, FOLD INTO sc5 instead. Likely also affects T23/T33 (same lat=1 leapr path). Files: src/processing/leapr.jl. Cross-ref NJOY_jl-sc5 (T80 FP-order residual), NJOY_jl-gm1 (coherent elastic).
acceptance_criteria: T09 tape24 MF7/MT4 S(a,b) value diffs root-caused; T09 toward bit-identical (or magnitude confirmed FP-order and folded into sc5).

title: ACER: read MT152/153 from post-purr PENDF and emit LUNR block JXS[23] (T87)
description: T87 (Pt-190 ENDF8.1, new pinned test) has real URR; purr produces MT152/153 in the PENDF, but acer_module hardcodes jxs[JXS_IURPT]=0 (ace_builder.jl:126) so the ACE URR probability-table (LUNR) block is never written. Ref tape41 has JXS[23]=693913 (nonzero). Fix: acer iopt=1 path reads MT152/153 from post-purr PENDF and emits the LUNR XSS block. Fortran ref: acer.f90 URR prob-table emission. Acceptance: T87 ACE LUNR block present + sized. Part of the ACER promotion epic (NJOY_jl-cnh); related to purr fidelity (NJOY_jl-gvs).
notes: TRIAGE 2026-06-02: LUNR block ~95 LOC, LOW regression risk (gated on MT153 presence; read_mt153 from post-purr PENDF + emit 6+nure*(1+6*nurb) words + JXS[23]; Fortran acefc.f90:5278-5329 read, 5947-5984 emit). BUT only ~3% of T87 gap (T87 Julia 107277 vs ref 272570 lines). T87 also needs photon production (NTRP=781), secondary-neutron AND/DLW (NR=24), MT filter, AND purr probability-table fidelity (bead gvs, currently zeros). DEEP — LUNR alone leaves T87 DIFFS.

title: groupr: ign=1 user-supplied group structure not read from input (T86)
description: T86 (Hf-177 TENDL2021, new pinned test) groupr deck uses ign=1 with 4 user-supplied groups. NJOY.jl GroupStructureId(1) throws -> falls back to LANL-30, so Julia emits 30 groups vs ref 4, diverging from MF1/MT451 line 3 onward. Fix: in src/orchestration/input_parser.jl parse_groupr, read ngn + egn boundary cards that follow the groupr card when ign==1; in src/orchestration/modules/groupr.jl _groupr_group_structure, honor the user grid. Fortran ref: groupr.f90 gengpn (ign=1 user-grid read). Acceptance: T86 groupr group count + boundaries match ref tape25.
notes: CYCLE 1 LANDED (T86 parsing + panel quadrature): ign=1 card6a/6b user_egn read (gengpn groupr.f90:4156-4165), card9a mfd=-1 + compound-mfd parse into GrouprActivationEntry (groupr.f90:625-713), _groupr_group_structure ign==1 branch. Plus REQUIRED panel Lobatto-2 quadrature port (getwtf/getsig/panel 5115-6091) for nz=nl=1/MF3/iwt2,3/lord=0 — old group_integrate linearized 1/E (g1 flux 10x low, never validated for bit-identical GENDF). getwtf sigfig(enext,6,1) step. Writer ng2=2 normal / 3 for 251/252/253/452/455/456; group constants format_endf_float extended=false. T86 tape25 lines 1-16 (dict + MF3/MT4 C2=0, 4 groups) BIT-IDENTICAL, first-diff now line 17. Regression-clean: T01 NUMERIC_PASS 32812/32962, T04 81/82, T11 unchanged; BONUS T15 tape91 MF3 now matches ref. Cycle 2 (ow6) = reconr MF10 write + groupr MF10 averaging for sections 2-5.
close_reason: T86 BIT_IDENTICAL 52/52 (Hf-177 TENDL2021): groupr ign=1 + card9a/compound-mfd + reconr MF10 write + groupr MF10 activation averaging. Commits 675fdb5 (Cycle 1) + this. Regression-clean.

title: groupr: card9a extended-residual format (mfd=-1) not parsed (T86)
description: T86 deck uses groupr card9a extended format (mfd=-1 trigger) to emit MF10 activation entries with IZAR*10+lfs encoding (e.g. fzam=72177.029). NJOY.jl parse_groupr reads mfd=-1 as a raw mt_list entry (no two-line card9a lookahead), and groupr_module only processes mfd==3, so all extended/MF10 sections are silently dropped. Fix: parse_groupr two-line card9a read on mfd<0; groupr_module MF10 activation averaging path. Fortran ref: groupr.f90 card9/9a + MF10. Acceptance: T86 MF10/extended sections present in tape25. Depends on the ign=1 fix.
notes: CYCLE 2 LANDED → T86 BIT_IDENTICAL 52/52. reconr _reconstruct_mf10 (reconr.jl/reconr_types.jl/pendf_writer.jl/pendf_io.jl): reconstruct every MF10 sub-section onto union grid, INT=2 lin interp, sigfig(7,0), gety1 thresh + ith trim (reconr.f90:4734/4792/4834/4918); GATED on MF10 presence (empty for non-activation mats → no-op). groupr.jl: per activation_entry, file==4 (MF10) → extract_mf10_subsections match by izar+lfs, group-average via the EXACT iwt=3 _groupr_panel_xs, emit MF3/MTd with fzam in C2 (izar+lfs/1000 card9a, or direct compound izam), igzero zero-group skip (groupr.f90:893) for threshold sections 4/5. Regression-clean: T01 NUMERIC_PASS 32812/32962, T02 tape28 12519/13873, T08 STRUCTURAL_FAIL unchanged, T04 81/82+56/74+107/119 unchanged.
close_reason: T86 BIT_IDENTICAL 52/52 (Hf-177 TENDL2021): groupr ign=1 + card9a/compound-mfd + reconr MF10 write + groupr MF10 activation averaging. Commits 675fdb5 (Cycle 1) + this. Regression-clean.

title: ACER aplots: port heating/threshold/particle-production blocks for T53/T54/T62
description: aplots base (page1/2 principal XS + aplof4 elastic perspective) is done (T50/T52 BIT_IDENTICAL). Remaining unported blocks raise AplotsNotPortedError and degrade tape33 to an empty stub for the richer charged tests: (1) log-log + lin-lin heating per reaction (acefc.f90:16177-16226, 16544-16595) — T53/T54 hit this; (2) aplotr threshold-reaction page (acefc.f90:16970-17092) — T62/T51/T71 hit this; (3) aploxp particle-production sections (acefc.f90 aploxp) + particle-heating-contributions page — T53. Port these to flip T53/T62 tape33 to BIT_IDENTICAL. tape34/35 already BI; pure function of correct ACE arrays.
notes: Phase 80 (2026-05-31): T53 + T62 tape33 now FULL BIT_IDENTICAL (17236/17236 + 5460/5460, cmp-clean). Ported aplotr threshold-reaction page, log-log + lin-lin heating pages, and aploxp (C1 particle-heating-contributions, C2 recoil, C3 production-XS, C5 3D angular reusing _aplof4_perspective!). Fixed _e14_6 to drop the E for 3-digit exponents. Zero regressions (T50/T52/T61). T54 tape33 now structurally complete (11340/11340) but DIFFS 1-ULP downstream of its tape34 ACE FP residual -> gated on bead NJOY_jl-53h (NOT aplots). Remaining aplots sub-paths (MT=444 damage, URR, aplonu, aplodn, aplopf, aplof4 equiprobable contour, aploxp law-4/44/61 emission) stay loud-guarded; no current test exercises them.
close_reason: aplots porting scope complete: T53 + T62 FULL BIT_IDENTICAL (Phase 80). T54's residual is its tape34 ACE FP (bead NJOY_jl-53h), not aplots. Unported aplots sub-paths remain loud-guarded and ungated by any current test; file a fresh bead if a future test needs one.

title: docs: shard HANDOFF.md into thin live doc + handoff_archive/
description: HANDOFF.md is 3886 lines / 337KB — exceeds the Read tool cap, has a STALE Phase-33-era '## Current State' that contradicts reality, two duplicate '## Immediate Next Steps' sections (lines 1139 and 2302), and ~830 lines of archival '## Session History' plus trailing dated session logs. Split into a thin live HANDOFF.md (~600-800 lines: project intro, rules, Beads status, Recent Phases table, Open Work, Grind Method, Architecture, Traps, ONE reconciled Next Steps, T01/gdb/key-files howtos, plus a NEW verified 'Current State (2026-05-30)') + handoff_archive/ holding the historical sections VERBATIM. Move, do not rewrite history. Preserve all worklog/*.md references. Model: Bennett.jl's thin-index + sharded-chunks discipline.
acceptance_criteria: HANDOFF.md thin (<900 lines), readable in one Read; every original '## ' section present in either thin or handoff_archive/; worklog references intact; stale Current State superseded; duplicate Next Steps reconciled; MANIFEST proves zero content loss.
close_reason: Doc-hygiene orchestration 2026-05-30: AGENTS.md re-synced byte-identical to CLAUDE.md (BennettVM pattern); HANDOFF.md sharded 3886->1314 lines + handoff_archive/ (0 sections dropped, verified); worklog/INDEX.md added (all 50 files). See session report.

title: docs: sync AGENTS.md <-> CLAUDE.md (BennettVM identical-file pattern) + drop contradictory beads block
description: AGENTS.md is 5.2KB of auto-generated beads boilerplate that does NOT mirror CLAUDE.md and actively contradicts this repo: it tells agents to 'bd dolt push' (no Dolt remote exists here), claims 'no manual export needed' (bd export -o IS required to land beads in .beads/issues.jsonl), and says 'do NOT use MEMORY.md files' (this repo's harness DOES use MEMORY.md + typed memory, per CLAUDE.md). Adopt the BennettVM.jl gold-standard pattern: AGENTS.md body identical to CLAUDE.md with a 'edit both in the same commit; never drift' header. Fold the useful non-interactive-shell tips (cp -f / rm -f / apt -y) into CLAUDE.md first so nothing is lost. Remove the injected BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION block (CLAUDE.md already documents the correct embedded-Dolt/JSONL/MEMORY.md model and even notes this block was deleted once for the same contradictions).
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md body == CLAUDE.md body (BennettVM pattern); no contradictory bd-dolt/no-export/no-MEMORY.md guidance; non-interactive-shell tips preserved in CLAUDE.md.
close_reason: Doc-hygiene orchestration 2026-05-30: AGENTS.md re-synced byte-identical to CLAUDE.md (BennettVM pattern); HANDOFF.md sharded 3886->1314 lines + handoff_archive/ (0 sections dropped, verified); worklog/INDEX.md added (all 50 files). See session report.

title: T22 leapr MF1/MT451 header one-field drift (4635/4636)
description: T22 (leapr para-H2 S(a,b)) was recorded BIT_IDENTICAL 4636/4636 but the 2026-05-30 sweep against fresh NJOY2016 develop (ac5adf5) shows 4635/4636. Exactly one line differs, line 4 of tape20 (MF1/MT451 header, MAT=2): REF '1.000000+0 7.590000+0 0 0 12 6' vs JUL '1.000000+0 0.000000+0 0 0 12 6'. The Julia code is an unchanged fresh clone and deterministically writes 0.0; HANDOFF recorded 4636/4636 against the OLD reference, so the upstream reference now emits 7.59 in this header field where it previously had 0.0 (an ENDF version/metadata field, not physics). Decide: (a) match the new reference field in the leapr MF1 writer, or (b) pin the reference to the validated baseline (see baseline-pin bead) and treat as upstream drift. Read leapr.f90 MF1/MT451 emission to determine what 7.59 is before changing anything (LAW 2: Fortran before Julia).
acceptance_criteria: T22 tape20 back to 4636/4636 against the pinned reference, OR documented as upstream drift with the field's meaning identified from leapr.f90.
notes: ROOT CAUSE (orchestrated 2026-06-01): NOT T22-specific. Upstream NJOY changed MF1/MT451 record-3 field-2 (EMAX) in the PENDF directory from 0.000000+0 to the real Emax. Affects 5 reference tapes identically between Dec-2025 and Mar-2026 develop: tests 09 (6.325-1), 22 (7.590+0), 23, 33 (1.002133+1), 80 (5.000001+0). Julia emits the OLD form (EMAX=0). To close against the pinned oracle 2c64dfb, the port's MF1/MT451 writer must emit the real EMAX for these modules (leapr T22/T80; verify 09/23/33). Verify which NJOY subroutine sets this field at the pinned commit before coding.
close_reason: FIXED: leapr MF1/MT451 record-3 EMAX 0.0 -> round_sigfig(0.0253*p.beta[end],7,0) at leapr_writer.jl:180, mirroring leapr.f90:3083 (upstream PR #372). T22 tape20 BIT_IDENTICAL 4636/4636 independently re-verified (cache-nuked). Same fix corrects EMAX line for all 5 tests (09/22/23/33/80); residual non-EMAX diffs tracked T80->sc5, T09->lho. 3 RED->GREEN unit assertions (test_leapr_writer.jl).

title: ACER: iopt=7 suffix rename + xsdir format (T61 flip)
description: T61 acer iopt=7 (rename existing thermal ACE hh2o to suffix 0.90). Bug1: Julia writes hh2o.99t, ref hh2o.90t — iopt=7 path doesn't apply card2 new-suffix 0.90. Bug2: xsdir line (tape72) column widths + temperature format differ (ref 2.530E-08, jul 2.5300E-08; ref '1 1   196795', jul '1   1   196795'). Fix _acer_iopt7 suffix application + the xsdir writer. Shared xsdir format likely also fixes T50/T52/T62 tape35 (all 0/1). Fortran acer.f90 iopt=7 path + xsdir/newdir writer. Acceptance: T61 tape71 49211/49211 + tape72 1/1; regression T50/T52/T62 tape34 BI, T08 xsdir.
close_reason: T61 BIT_IDENTICAL (tape71 49211/49211, tape72 1/1). Fixed: (1) iopt=7 now applies card-2 suff (acer.f90:295) via newsuff port (acecm.f90:619) -> hh2o.99t->hh2o.90t; (2) class-aware xsdir line writer: thermal i2,' 1 ',i9 (aceth.f90:2422) vs neutron i2,i4,1x,i8 (acefc.f90:13013), temp 1pe10.3. BONUS: T50/T52/T62 tape35 xsdir flipped 0/1->1/1 BI (tape34 unchanged BI). Regression-clean: T08 neutron unchanged.

title: ACER: T62 (d+He3) tape34 61-line value grind -> BIT_IDENTICAL
description: Post-ptlegc T62 tape34 DIFFS 7160/7221, line count exact (7221=7221). 61 value-differing lines. T62 is d+He3 = non-identical particles (LIDP=0), so this likely exercises the coul LIDP=0 Coulomb branch that T52 (LIDP=1, BIT_IDENTICAL) did not. Grind-Method: first differing line on tape34 -> root cause -> match Fortran FP. Fortran coul/ptlegc/acecpe (acefc.f90:7980-8201, acecpe ~6529-6589). Regression guards: T52 3986/3986, T50 163/163.
close_reason: T62 tape34 BIT_IDENTICAL 7221/7221 (verified). Root causes (all deep, all cited): (1) MF3 interp law dropped for charged INT=5/6 reactions (MT=600 INT=6 Coulomb penetrability) -> new _parse_mf3_tab1/extract_mf3_tab1_all carry NBT/INT+QM/QI, acer samples with native law (terp1 i=6, endf.f90:1634); (2) disappear column = sum of sigfig-7 reaction xs mt<=200||mt>=600 (acelod acefc.f90:5648), not max(total-elastic,0); (3) heating zeroed for izai<=2004 (acefc.f90:6663); (4) LQR = QI/1e6 MeV; (5) a11 e20.11 drops E for |exp|>=100 (acecm.f90:791). Regression-clean: T52 3986/3986, T50 163/163, T02 tape28 12519/13873 NUMERIC_PASS unchanged, T08 unchanged.

title: thermr: T70 lat=10 coherent-elastic MF3 grid over-production (213800 vs 112817)
description: lat=10 read path (read_mf7_mt2) landed; T70 tape60 now emits the coherent-elastic section but the MF3 energy grid is ~1.9x over-produced (Julia 213800 lines vs ref 112817), only 1 line matches. Likely build_thermal_grid over-refines the 568 Al-27 Bragg edges, or the lat=10 BraggData (scon=1, no ulim sentinel, n_edges=NP) feeds the adaptive grid differently than Fortran coh. Diagnose by tallying MF3 per-MT line counts Julia vs ref (re-run needed; T70 is ~206s/iter). Fortran: coh adaptive merge (thermr.f90 ~429+) + sigcoh lat=10 (1150-1166). NOTE T70 also has non-elastic (inelastic S(a,b)) diffs per scoping — full NUMERIC_PASS needs both.
notes: TRIAGE 2026-06-02: BEAD PREMISE STALE. Julia tape60 213800 lines == LOCAL ref 213800 (the '112817' came from a different/older machine clone). NOT grid over-production. Real blocker: Julia writes a CORRUPT MF1/MT451 header on the thermr output — LRP=0 (should 2), NFOR=0 (should 6), and line-4 CONT is thermr temp/tol (4.0e2/1.0e-3) instead of AWI/EMAX/NSUB/NVER (1.0/1.5e8/10/8) — diverges from line 2, fails all lines. Also minor: lat=10 missing emax nudge-down seed sigfig(emax,7,-1) in build_thermal_grid (~1 pt, ~3 LOC). Full T70 pass also needs inelastic S(a,b) calcem grind. Re-scope: fix the MF1/MT451 LRP/NFOR writer first (regression-test T01/T02/T08). DEEP overall.

title: thermr: wire incoherent-elastic (iel, LTHR=2) into pipeline
description: incoh_elastic_xs() exists in thermr.jl but is never called. Fortran coh dispatch sets icoh=10*lthr; LTHR=2 -> icoh=20 -> iel(20,...) (thermr.f90:428-434, iel ~1288-1424). Wire it for LTHR=2 (and the iel half of LTHR=3). Unblocks the elastic section of T69 (Zr-in-ZrH), T74 (H-in-ZrH), and the iel half of T67 (D-in-LiD). Acceptance: those tests gain a real incoherent-elastic section vs ref.

title: ACER: photon-production + particle-type blocks (NTRP>0/NTYPE>0)
description: Port GPD/MTRP/LSIGP/SIGP/LANDP/ANDP/LDLWP/DLWP + PTYPE/NTRO/PLOCT for charged-particle iopt=1. Depends on ptlegc. Acceptance: T53,T54 NUMERIC_PASS.
notes: SCOPE CORRECTED (Fortran-verified 2026-05-29): T54 has NTRP=0, NTYPE=4 — it needs the PARTICLE-TYPE blocks (acelcp, acefc.f90:9121-11056 + first() 230-800), NOT photon production. H-3 ENDF has no MF12-15. Blocks: PTYPE/NTRO/PLOCT + per-type HPD/MTRH/TYRH/LSIGH/SIGH/LANDH/ANDH/LDLWH/DLWH/YH. ~520 LOC. ptleg2/pttab2 already exist in ace_charged.jl. HIGH-risk: LAW=2 Legendre ANDH (~300 LOC, dominant), LAW=4 recoil back-read, YH production heating. T54 ref tape34 NXS: len2=29259 nes=140 ntr=2 ntype=4; JXS ptype=5445 ntro=5449 ploct=5453. Split Bead A (HPD+SIG+AND ~350) / Bead B (DLW+YH ~170). True photon-prod (acelpp NTRP>0) is a SEPARATE need (check which tests actually have MF12-15).
close_reason: acelcp particle-type port landed (new files ace_lcp.jl/ace_lcp_build.jl): PTYPE/NTRO/PLOCT + per-type HPD/MTRH/TYRH/LSIGH/SIGH/LANDH/ANDH/LDLWH/DLWH/YH for MF6 LAW=2/3/4 + MT=2 recoil. T53 tape34 BIT_IDENTICAL 12030/12030; T54 STRUCTURAL_FAIL->DIFFS (7327/7327 structure, 6383 match, tape35 BI). Also fixed 2 prereq bugs (charged union grid, SIG threshold index, both gated to charged path). T54 FP residual + unported LAWs tracked in new beads.

notes: TRIAGE 2026-06-02: suffix fix is a clean 2-LINE change (input_parser.jl:298 and :326 '80c'->'00c'; Fortran acer.f90:292 suff=0 default -> zaid '.00'). Zero regression (T50/52/53/54/61/62/71 all set explicit suffix; only T08+T14 use default). BUT flips ZERO tests: it fixes 1 ZAID line; T14 needs ~300-500 LOC more (MF6 LAW=1 Kalbach in acelcp NTYPE=3, photon-production NTRP, secondary-neutron AND/DLW NR=1). Land the 2-line fix as a correctness freebie; T14 milestone is DEEP.
close_reason: Fix landed in commit 0da9fa2 (input_parser.jl:298,326 default suffix 80c→00c, Fortran acer.f90:292 suff=0→.00). Verified present in working tree. Correctness freebie; T14 milestone is a separate deep MF6 LAW=1 task.

title: thermr: read MF7/MT2 Bragg lattice from ENDF (remove hardcoded table)
description: Replace hardcoded BRAGG_LATTICE_PARAMS with per-material MF7/MT2 reads. Unblocks T25,T67-70,T74 (6-7 thermal tests on stubs). Fortran thermr.f90 bragg. Files: src/processing/thermr.jl, src/orchestration/auto_params.jl.
acceptance_criteria: BRAGG_LATTICE_PARAMS removed; T25,T67,T68,T69,T70,T74 use real MF7/MT2 reads.
notes: PREMISE CORRECTED (verified vs Fortran 2026-05-28): do NOT 'replace the hardcoded table'. Fortran sigcoh (thermr.f90:947-1207) supports BOTH: lat=1/2/3 = hardcoded graphite/Be/BeO (NJOY.jl BRAGG_LATTICE_PARAMS is FAITHFUL to these — keep them), and lat=10 = read reciprocal-lattice/Bragg data from ENDF-6 MF7/MT2 ('if(lat.eq.10) go to 200', thermr.f90:1016; coh caller picks coh(10,..) when MF7/MT2 present, thermr.f90:429/433). Correct scope: ADD the lat=10 MF7/MT2 coherent-elastic read path (port sigcoh's go-to-200 branch + the coh dispatch), KEEP the built-in lattices. T25/T67-70/T74 likely need lat=10. Re-verify which lat each of those tests uses before coding.

Also fixed latent read_mf7_mt4 bug: natom was read from B(4) (=emax 2.277->2 for Al, a /2 bound-XS error) instead of the input card (thermr.f90:1670-1676). T01 regression-clean (32812/32962, byte-identical -> natom no-op for H-in-H2O). Output-grid fidelity for T70 tracked in new grid-over-production bead; iel/LTHR=2 in NJOY_jl-69j.

title: Sweep reporter: flag substantive-bit-identical tests blocked only by a known-stub tape
description: The sweep classifier (test/validation/sweep_reference_tests.jl, test_status_symbol) marks a TEST bit-identical only if EVERY referenceTape passes. Four ACER tests (T50/T52/T53/T62) are byte-perfect on their substantive ACE tape (tape34) and xsdir (tape35) but carry an unimplemented aplots plot tape (tape33, 0/0), so they report as DIFFS and the headline reads '2 BIT_IDENTICAL' when ~6 tests are substantively bit-identical. This nearly caused a misread of the port's health this session. Add a status tier (e.g. SUBSTANTIVE_BIT_IDENTICAL or a per-tape 'KNOWN_STUB' annotation) so the headline reflects real correctness and known-stub tapes don't mask wins. Keep the strict full-test BIT_IDENTICAL count too. Serves the north-star 'more pleasant to work with' goal.
acceptance_criteria: Sweep report distinguishes 'all substantive tapes bit-identical, only known-stub tape failing' from genuine DIFFS; headline counts both.

title: ACER: acelcp unported MF6 LAWs (1/6/7/Kalbach/phase-space/MF4)

Needed for T51 (MF6/MT=28 LAW=6 phase-space) and likely T14/T71. Port the missing LAW renderers. Blocks T51 particle-production + others.

title: ACER: T54 tape34 FP residual to BIT_IDENTICAL (6383/7327)
description: acelcp structure is correct (7327/7327 lines). Residual: ~2000 words at ~7e-7 (pre-existing acer_charged_elastic Coulomb total/elastic 1-ULP 7-sigfig noise, same FP-floor class as T01/T34, exposed now grid matches) + 1 word >1e-5 (triton MT=2 recoil heating[1] at E=100eV: ref=0 exactly, jul=6.008e-6 — recoil ubar FP-accumulation must clamp below delt). Grind these to flip T54 to NUMERIC_PASS/BI.
design: ROOT CAUSE (diagnosed 2026-06-01, NOT yet oracle-verified): triton MT=2 recoil heating[1]=6.008e-6 (ref=0 exactly) comes from _terpa_scr2 (src/formats/ace_lcp_build.jl:601) extrapolating the below-range region to Vi(1) when e<=Ei(1). Fortran terpa returns y=0 for x below the first tabulated point (endf.f90:1812-1816, label 170). First ESZ energy (~1e-5 eV) is below first heating-kernel energy Ei(1), so ref=0 but Julia returns Vi(1)*prodxs. The delt=1e-10 clamp at line 240 cannot catch 6e-6. FIX (not applied): change line 601 to 'e < Ei(1) && return 0.0'. THEN reproduce-first per Law 1 (reference_test.jl 54), apply, verify T54 DIFFS->NUMERIC_PASS, regression-check T53 (shares acelcp, currently FULL BI). The ~2000-word Coulomb 7e-7 residual (BIT_IDENTICAL stretch) is separate/untouched. See worklog/T54_recoil_heating_terpa_diagnosis.md
notes: PROGRESS 2026-06-02 (orchestrated, 2 commits pushed 648c6cc+8c622c7): tape34 DIFFS -> NUMERIC_PASS via TWO real bugs. (1) recoil below-range: ace_lcp_build.jl:601 'e<=Ei(1) return Vi(1)' -> 'e<Ei(1) return 0.0' (Fortran terpa label 170, endf.f90:1812-1817). (2) AND-block LC locators integer-format: ace_lcp_build.jl:502 added isint=true (acefc.f90:9520 xss(nb+ie)=-(next-andh+1); writer i20 via acecm typen iflag=1). tape34 1e-9 6383->6398, NUMERIC_PASS 7326/7327 (only wildcarded date). T53/50/52/62 FULL BI preserved, T02/T08 unchanged. REMAINING BLOCKER (T54 test still DIFFS): HANDOFF claim 'tape33 flips once recoil word lands' WRONG. tape33 DIFFS 9919/11340 - ALL diffs are charged heating/elastic ~7e-7 FP-floor (T01/T34 class), with 4 outliers >1e-5 (max 1.84e-5 at L3109/3115/3130/3132, E=3.1-6.6 MeV) in the 'recoil heating' PLOT curve which aplots computes as esz_heat - Sum(particle_heating) -> subtractive cancellation amplifies the underlying 7e-7 ACE-heating residual to 1.8e-5. tape34 949 lines off @1e-9 = same residual. To flip T54->NUMERIC_PASS: reduce those 4 plot outliers <1e-5 (either match Fortran aplots esz_heat-Sum order in ace_aplots.jl, OR reduce the 7e-7 charged-heating residual). To flip T54->BI: kill the 7e-7 residual entirely. Deferred as deep FP grind; recoil+locator portion DONE.

title: MF32/rescon: LCOMP=0 (rpxlc0) + LCOMP=2 (rpxlc2) + NRO!=0 + NLRS!=0 readers
description: MISSING (currently @warn+skipped, so no resonance-param cov for these evaluations). Complements NJOY_jl-4pz (rpxsamm, LRF=4/7): this covers the OTHER skipped MF32 sub-formats — LCOMP=0 legacy (Fortran rpxlc0 errorr.f90:3734), LCOMP=2 compact (rpxlc2 errorr.f90:4634), energy-dependent scattering radius NRO!=0, and long-range cov NLRS!=0 (Fortran rpxlc12 itself aborts on NLRS>0, errorr.f90:4618). Files: src/processing/mf32_reader.jl (the @warn+skip sites), src/processing/rescon.jl.
acceptance_criteria: UNBOUNDED — true scope is unknown until the Fortran is read. FIRST work step when picked up: a scoping pass (read the cited .f90, enumerate sub-features + which reference tests need each) that FILES ONE CHILD BEAD per sub-feature/test. This epic closes only when its children close. Do NOT attempt a monolithic implementation. Evaluations using LCOMP=0/2 (or NRO/NLRS) get faithful MF32->MF33 rescon contributions matching reference.

title: LEAPR coherent elastic (iel>0 MF7/MT2) + secondary scatterer (nss>0) + Skold (nsk=2)
description: MISSING. leapr_module guards these as stubs (@warn 'iel/nss not yet ported'; 'skold nsk=2 not wired'). Inelastic S(a,b) (T22 bit-identical, T80 numeric) works; the coherent-elastic MF7/MT2 emission, secondary-scatterer mixing, and Skold intermolecular coherence are absent. Affects e.g. T23 (BeO, secondary scatterer + iel=3): tape20 4/16948. Fortran: njoy-reference/src/leapr.f90 (coh/skold/secondary, endout MF7/MT2). Files: src/orchestration/modules/leapr.jl, src/processing/leapr.jl (skold! exists but unwired), leapr_writer.jl (MF7/MT2 path). Distinct from sc5 (FP-order).
acceptance_criteria: UNBOUNDED — true scope is unknown until the Fortran is read. FIRST work step when picked up: a scoping pass (read the cited .f90, enumerate sub-features + which reference tests need each) that FILES ONE CHILD BEAD per sub-feature/test. This epic closes only when its children close. Do NOT attempt a monolithic implementation. T23 and other coherent-elastic/secondary-scatterer S(a,b) tests produce MF7/MT2 + reach NUMERIC_PASS.

title: POWR lib=2 (EPRI-CPM CLIB) + lib=3 — currently error('not yet ported')
description: MISSING. powr_module supports lib=1 (EPRI-CELL fast/GAMTAP, bit-identical on several oracles) only; lib=2 (EPRI-CPM, CLIB binary) and lib=3 error out ('powr lib=N not yet ported'). Each is a distinct library format. Fortran: njoy-reference/src/powr.f90 (cpm/therm paths). Files: src/orchestration/modules/powr.jl, src/formats/powr.jl (EPRIThermal/CPM types already declared).
acceptance_criteria: UNBOUNDED — true scope is unknown until the Fortran is read. FIRST work step when picked up: a scoping pass (read the cited .f90, enumerate sub-features + which reference tests need each) that FILES ONE CHILD BEAD per sub-feature/test. This epic closes only when its children close. Do NOT attempt a monolithic implementation. powr lib=2 and lib=3 produce reference-matching libraries for their tests.

title: CCCCR: wire GENDF -> ISOTXS/BRKOXS/DLAYXS (module is a stub)
description: MISSING. The CCCC binary writers exist (src/formats/ccccr.jl, ccccr_b.jl: write_isotxs/brkoxs/dlayxs) but src/orchestration/modules/ccccr.jl is a marker-text stub — it never parses the GENDF input or feeds the writers ('GENDF format is complex; full parsing not yet implemented'). Sweep fingerprint: T56-59/T64/T66/T78 emit 0-line tapes (tape31/34/37 0/0). Fortran: njoy-reference/src/ccccr.f90 (cisotx/cbrkxs/cdlyxs + its GENDF reader).
acceptance_criteria: UNBOUNDED — true scope is unknown until the Fortran is read. FIRST work step when picked up: a scoping pass (read the cited .f90, enumerate sub-features + which reference tests need each) that FILES ONE CHILD BEAD per sub-feature/test. This epic closes only when its children close. Do NOT attempt a monolithic implementation. All ccccr reference tests (T56-59/T64/T66/T78) produce non-empty ISOTXS/BRKOXS/DLAYXS matching reference.

title: errorr: port rpxsamm for LRF=7/LRF=4 MF32 resonance-param cov (proper T20 fix)
description: Proper fix for T20 (and other SAMMY/RML + Adler evaluations): port Fortran rpxsamm (errorr.f90:3252+) so read_mf32/rescon compute MF32->MF33 sandwich contributions for LRF=7 (R-Matrix Limited) and LRF=4 (Adler-Adler), not just LRF=1/2/3 (BW/RM) + URR. Depends on the graceful-skip interim landing first.
acceptance_criteria: T20 covariance includes LRF=7 rescon contributions matching reference.

title: broadr: sigma1.jl:67 ntuple(5)->ntuple(Val(5)) (eliminate per-interval closure alloc)
description: h_all's h=ntuple(5) do k (sigma1.jl:67) heap-allocs a closure per interval (~239 B/call, O(nseg^2) total over a broadr run). ntuple(f,Val(5)) forces compile-time unroll, eliminating it. Verified bit-identical over 20k tuples (max diff 0.0); matches codebase convention (Val(62) faddeeva_table.jl, Val(50) sigma1.jl). Not needed for <300s (already met) but a free GC win. Optionally also h_taylor MVector{50} (rare path).
acceptance_criteria: sigma1.jl:67 uses Val(5); T01 tape25 stays 32812/32962 byte-identical; broadr GC pressure reduced.
close_reason: Landed: sigma1.jl:67 ntuple(5)->ntuple(Val(5)). Byte-identical (T01 tape25 32812/32962). Eliminates per-interval closure alloc.

title: Grind remaining DIFFS per-tape (Grind Method, after fresh sweep)
description: Per-tape bisection via the Grind Method on remaining DIFFS once the post-Phase-76 sweep baseline exists. Each tape: diff cols 1-66, classify, read Fortran, fix.
acceptance_criteria: Each targeted DIFFS tape -> NUMERIC_PASS or better.

title: purr: probability-table value fidelity (T28,T35-42,T63,T72)
description: MT=152/153 structurally wired (Phase 38) but T38 STRUCTURAL_FAIL 3642 vs ref 3480. Real MC ladder value-fidelity work. Files: src/processing/purr.jl, purr_writer.jl.
acceptance_criteria: At least one purr test reaches NUMERIC_PASS @1e-5.

title: reconr: T49 MLBW +/-1 at E=110487.7 eV (MT=1, MT=2)
description: T49 RECONR 44/46 PERFECT; MT=1/MT=2 differ +/-1 last sigfig at single E=110487.7 eV. Same class as T34 Frobenius-Schur fix (always a real bug). gdb-trace Fortran csmlbw intermediates. Files: src/resonances/mlbw.jl; Fortran reconr.f90 csmlbw ~2988-3023.
acceptance_criteria: T49 reports 46/46 PERFECT, no +/-1 at E=110487.7 eV.

title: errorr: cross-ign flux-weighted collapse (colaps)
description: Fortran colaps (errorr.f90:9255-9283) flux-weighted sum over fine groups into coarse. Not blocking current tests (T15/16/17 same ign). Build _errorr_colaps when a coarser-output test needs it. Files: src/orchestration/modules/errorr.jl.
acceptance_criteria: A coarser-output-grid test produces flux-weighted sigma matching reference.

title: errorr: T65 MF34 performance (re-measure first)
description: T65 errorr MF=34 angular-dist cov slow on sweep watchlist; may be subsumed by Phase 44 fix. Re-measure before optimizing. Files: src/orchestration/modules/errorr.jl MF=34 path.
acceptance_criteria: T65 completes under sweep default timeout with real output.

title: plotr: real plot-tape output (currently stub, T06)
description: plotr is a Phase 11 dispatch stub. Pure plot-tape, no downstream chain. Lowest priority. Files: src/orchestration/modules/plotr.jl.
acceptance_criteria: plotr produces a real plot tape for T06.

title: groupr: T04 tape25 nubar ULP (already passes 1e-7)
description: MF3/MT452 nubar 1-unit-in-7th-digit at LANL group 10 FP boundary; tape25 passes @1e-7 already. _groupr_nubar_records panel order. Demoted. HANDOFF P3.
acceptance_criteria: T04 tape25 119/119 at no-tolerance (needs gdb panel-order match).
